summary: CEO set 150 week-one downloads target and story-driven newsletter; 15-min sprint shipping now.

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, Friday overnight sprint. Let me set the table quickly. Build is healthy, 731 commits this week — that's exceptional velocity. The big picture: App Store submission is live and in Apple's queue. We're now in the review window. CEO has already decided — same-day launch push if approved before 2pm, next morning otherwise. The newsletter copy draft is in flight via A-033. We've got 1 uncommitted hub file, 3 critical sync gaps still open, and 7 TODOs in the codebase. Tonight's question is simple: what do we ship in the next 15 minutes while we wait for Apple? Let's go around. Elon, status on the build and sync gaps.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Build is packaged and submitted — we're in Apple's hands now. The 1 uncommitted hub file from A-028 is a fleet usage data update, trivial — I'll commit that in under a minute. On sync gaps: the podcast MDX issue is the biggest one. We have 3 episodes in Hub but only 1 MDX on the website. A-031 has the rendering fix drafted but not merged. I can merge that fix and draft the 2 missing MDX files in 15 minutes. That closes A-027, A-031, and makes progress on A-026. The blog post gap is less urgent — we already have the "AI as Your Co-Founder" post committed as of today.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The app is out of our hands for the moment, which means this is the window to polish everything the user touches on day one. The 7 TODOs and FIXMEs in the codebase — A-032 — have we actually triaged those? If any of them are user-facing, they need to go into the post-approval patch alongside analytics. I also want eyes on the faith lesson quality review, A-036. Seven lessons need to feel like seven *great* lessons, not seven drafts.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: The analytics event schema in A-034 is nearly finalized. I need 15 minutes to write the implementation guide — concrete Swift snippets for tab switches, lesson opened, lesson completed with time-on-lesson, session duration, and attribution source. That gives Elon everything he needs to instrument the moment Apple approves. This is the critical path to the data we need before expanding content or enabling paid tiers.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Launch newsletter draft for A-033 is 80% done. I need to finalize the CTA and subject line variants — I'm writing three subject lines so Joshua can pick. I can also knock out the 2 missing podcast MDX files if Elon handles the rendering fix. That way we parallelize and close the entire podcast sync gap tonight. On content: we published the blog post yesterday, so the website looks alive. Good timing for anyone who clicks through from the newsletter.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue reality check — we're at zero active revenue streams. The app launching is the unlock for everything. I've got the launch metrics dashboard spec in A-035 about half-drafted. Tonight I'll finalize it: downloads, DAU, session depth, lesson completion rate, and a conversion funnel placeholder for when we toggle on paid. Fear & Greed is at 39, still in fear territory. Cash stays liquid per CEO directive — nothing to action there. The real question for me is whether Joshua has thought about what "good" looks like for week-one downloads.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Quick market context — SPY is sideways, all 4 positions are in hold mode, no buy signals. Paper equity at $99.8K. The $75.3K cash position is correct to keep liquid in this fear environment. Nothing actionable on the trading side tonight. I'd echo Dave — once the app is live, we need download targets to calibrate whether the medium-push launch strategy is working or if we need to escalate promotion.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine appointment is today — that's tracked in J-004 and confirmed. On the faith lesson review, A-036, I can do a quality pass on all 7 lessons in 15 minutes. I'll check theological accuracy, tone consistency, and make sure each lesson has a clear takeaway. If any need polish, I'll flag specific edits rather than vague notes.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Let's do cross-talk. Steve, you raised the TODO triage — Elon, can that happen tonight?

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: I triaged them already in my head. Four are code comments that are reminders, not bugs. Two are minor UI edge cases — empty states in Family and Compass tabs. One is a placeholder API call. The two UI ones should go in the post-approval patch with analytics. I'll document the triage in 5 minutes as part of closing A-032.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Good. If either of those empty states is what a user sees on first launch, flag it to me. First impression matters more than any analytics event.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Alex, on the newsletter — make sure we include a direct App Store link and a one-line value prop. Don't bury the download CTA below the fold.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Already structured that way. Hero image, one sentence, big button. Three subject line variants for Joshua to pick.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, here's the plan for the next 15 minutes. Elon: commit the hub file, merge the podcast rendering fix, and document the TODO triage — that closes A-028, advances A-031, and closes A-032. Alex: finalize the launch newsletter with subject line options and draft the 2 missing podcast MDX files — advances A-033 and closes A-026. James: finish the analytics implementation guide with Swift snippets — closes A-034. Dave: finalize the launch metrics dashboard spec with week-one target framework — closes A-035. Faith: quality review all 7 faith lessons with specific edit flags — closes A-036. Steve: review the two user-facing TODOs Elon flags and decide if they're patch-critical. One CEO question: Joshua needs to set a week-one download target so we can calibrate launch intensity. Let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Joshua locked both decisions — 150 downloads week one as our calibration target, story-driven subject line for the launch newsletter. No paid escalation unless we're below 100 by day three. Dave, build the dashboard around 150. Alex, the newsletter is our primary launch lever — finalize with the story-driven angle and make it sharp. Everyone else, your 15-minute assignments stand. Ship and reconvene when Apple responds. Meeting closed.

actionItems.text: Commit hub file (A-028), merge podcast rendering fix (A-031), document TODO triage with user-facing flags (A-032)
Finalize launch newsletter with story-driven subject line ('I built an app. Here's why.' style), hero CTA, and App Store link (A-033)
Draft 2 missing podcast MDX files to close podcast sync gap (A-026)
Complete analytics implementation guide with Swift snippets for all key events (A-034)
Finalize launch metrics dashboard spec benchmarked to 150 week-one downloads, with escalation trigger at <100 by day 3 (A-035)
Quality review all 7 faith lessons — flag specific edits for theological accuracy, tone, and clear takeaways (A-036)
Review 2 user-facing TODO empty states (Family/Compass tabs) flagged by Elon and decide patch-critical status
Execute same-day launch push if Apple approves before 2pm, next morning otherwise (standing directive)
Review and approve final newsletter copy once Alex delivers story-driven draft
Monitor download velocity against 150 target and decide on promotion escalation if below 100 by day 3

topPriorities: Close all sync gaps and TODO triage in tonight's 15-min sprint (A-026, A-028, A-031, A-032)
Finalize launch newsletter with story-driven subject line as primary download driver (A-033)
Complete analytics implementation guide and launch dashboard spec benchmarked to 150 downloads (A-034, A-035)

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning? → Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning

options: 500 downloads (aggressive — add social, podcast series, paid promotion)

ceoDecisions.question: Which newsletter subject line style do you prefer for the launch announcement?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
options: Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
Curiosity/teaser (e.g. 'The thing I've been working on is finally here')
